metadata.paper_title: Improving Quality Attributes of a Complex System Through Architectural Analysis - A Case Study
metadata.venue: Proceedings of the Ninth Annual IEEE International Conference and Workshop on the Engineering of Computer-Based Systems (ECBS'02)

system_context.system_description: A system to manage nuclear simulations where input data is edited and stored in a database, simulation programs are executed on Unix servers, and results are analyzed via a client.
system_context.business_goals: Optimize plant maintenance, ensure safety, and improve efficiency and quality of simulation work.

evaluation_setup.evaluation_goal: To find the best architectural solution using different variants and comparing them to ensure robustness, maintainability, and acceptable performance.

architectures.name: Client Processing / Distributed Storage
architectures.description: Files are processed in the client; files are stored on the node where simulation took place (distributed).

key_components.responsibilities: Collects/edits input data, presents output files, processes/filters files.
Maintains one simulation; spawns TCS processes.
Responsible for the execution of one simulation task.

assumptions: There is exactly one SB per server computer.

architectures.name: Client Processing / Central Storage
architectures.description: Files are processed in the client; files are stored on one node acting as file server (centralized).

architectures.name: Server Processing / Distributed Storage
architectures.description: Files are processed on a server; files are stored distributed.

key_components.responsibilities: Processes input and output files associated with simulations on the server before transferring to client.

architectures.name: Server Processing / Central Storage
architectures.description: Files are processed on a server; files are stored centrally.

scenarios.scenario_text: PAM is extended with functionality to compare binary output files
A text file from a server computer is viewed in a client

scenario_classification.rationale: Explicitly identified as a 'use case' in the text, meaning it is supported by the current architecture without modification.
Explicitly identified as a 'change scenario' involving the 'addition of functionality'.
Described as a 'user scenario' used for performance analysis, implying execution on the existing system.
Described as a 'user scenario' used for performance analysis, implying execution on the existing system.
Described as a 'user scenario' used for performance analysis, implying execution on the existing system.

issues_identified: Architecture B2 clearly performed worst (between one and two whole transfers of a file).
Include one transfer, not necessarily of the whole file.

scenario_interactions.modularity_implication: Affected by 2 scenarios in A1/A2, which is undesirable because of its central position and robustness requirements.
Affected by 10 or 11 scenarios; high coupling.
Affected by 7 or 8 scenarios in B1/B2.

architectural_strengths: A1 and A2 have the lowest number of processes.
Socket mechanism and component independence contribute to robustness.
architectural_weaknesses: B2 performs worst regarding large data transfers.
B1 and B2 have significantly more processes due to SDS.
A1 and A2 have scenarios affecting the critical SB component.

architecture_comparison.rationale: Superior maintainability stats; better performance than B2; fewer processes than B variants. A1/A2 selected as best (synthesized).
Superior maintainability stats; equal to A1 in most respects. A1/A2 selected as best (synthesized).
Inferior with respect to number of processes; requires more coding (SDS).
Inferior with respect to number of large data transfers; requires more coding (SDS).

recommendations: Postpone the tradeoff decision regarding file storage (Central vs Distributed) to the configuration phase.

missing_information: Full text of all 19 scenarios (only examples provided).
Specific authors list beyond Rikard Land (paper mentions 'We', but only one author listed at top).
inference_notes: Inferred 'Indirect' classification for scenarios described as 'change scenarios'.
Grouped A1 and A2 as rank 1 jointly as the paper concludes they are the choice and can be synthesized.
